docs.title: SCPI Instrument Toolkit — Python API
docs.text: Python drivers for controlling lab instruments — oscilloscopes, power supplies, multimeters, function generators, and more — over VISA (USB, GPIB, Serial, LAN).

docs.title: Install
docs.text:
pip install git+https://github.com/T-O-M-Tool-Oauto-Mationator/scpi-instrument-toolkit.git

Requires NI-VISA

Install NI-VISA for USB and GPIB communication. The toolkit uses PyVISA with the pyvisa-py backend, but NI-VISA provides the best hardware compatibility.

docs.title: Quickstart
docs.text:
from lab_instruments import HP_E3631A, HP_34401A

# Connect
psu = HP_E3631A("GPIB0::5::INSTR")
dmm = HP_34401A("GPIB0::22::INSTR")
psu.connect()
dmm.connect()

try:
    # Set 3.3 V on the +6 V channel
    psu.set_output_channel("positive_6_volts_channel", 3.3, current_limit=0.1)
    psu.enable_output(True)

    # Measure with the DMM
    dmm.configure_dc_voltage()
    voltage = dmm.read()
    print(f"Measured: {voltage} V")
finally:
    psu.enable_output(False)
    psu.disconnect()
    dmm.disconnect()

docs.title: PSU + DMM: Voltage Verification
docs.text:
Set a voltage on the power supply and verify it with a multimeter.

from lab_instruments import HP_E3631A, HP_34401A

psu = HP_E3631A("GPIB0::5::INSTR")
dmm = HP_34401A("GPIB0::22::INSTR")
psu.connect()
dmm.connect()

try:
    # Set PSU to 3.3V
    psu.set_output_channel("positive_6_volts_channel", 3.3, current_limit=0.5)
    psu.enable_output(True)

    # Measure with DMM
    dmm.configure_dc_voltage()
    measured = dmm.read()
    expected = 3.3

    error = measured - expected
    print(f"Set:      {expected:.3f} V")
    print(f"Measured: {measured:.6f} V")
    print(f"Error:    {error * 1000:.3f} mV")
finally:
    psu.enable_output(False)
    psu.disconnect()
    dmm.disconnect()

docs.title: Voltage Sweep with Data Collection
docs.text:
Sweep a power supply through voltages and log DMM readings.

import time
import csv
from lab_instruments import HP_E3631A, HP_34401A

psu = HP_E3631A("GPIB0::5::INSTR")
dmm = HP_34401A("GPIB0::22::INSTR")
psu.connect()
dmm.connect()

voltages = [1.0, 2.0, 3.3, 5.0]
results = []

try:
    psu.enable_output(True)
    dmm.configure_dc_voltage()

    for target_v in voltages:
        psu.set_output_channel("positive_6_volts_channel", target_v, current_limit=0.5)
        time.sleep(0.5)  # Settle time

        measured_v = dmm.read()
        measured_i = psu.measure_current("positive_6_volts_channel")
        results.append({"target": target_v, "measured_v": measured_v, "current": measured_i})
        print(f"{target_v:.1f} V -> {measured_v:.6f} V, {measured_i:.4f} A")

    # Export to CSV
    with open("sweep_results.csv", "w", newline="") as f:
        writer = csv.DictWriter(f, fieldnames=["target", "measured_v", "current"])
        writer.writeheader()
        writer.writerows(results)
    print("Saved to sweep_results.csv")
finally:
    psu.enable_output(False)
    psu.disconnect()
    dmm.disconnect()

docs.title: AWG + Scope: Signal Check
docs.text:
Output a sine wave from the AWG and measure it on the oscilloscope.

import time
from lab_instruments import Keysight_EDU33212A, Rigol_DHO804

awg = Keysight_EDU33212A("USB0::0x2A8D::0x1602::MY12345678::INSTR")
scope = Rigol_DHO804("USB0::0x1AB1::0x044C::DHO8A000000::INSTR")
awg.connect()
scope.connect()

try:
    # Output 1 kHz sine, 2 Vpp
    awg.set_waveform(1, "SIN", frequency=1000, amplitude=2.0)
    awg.enable_output(1, True)
    time.sleep(0.5)

    # Measure on scope
    scope.enable_channel(1)
    scope.autoset()
    time.sleep(1.0)

    freq = scope.measure_frequency(1)
    vpp = scope.measure_vpp(1)
    print(f"Frequency: {freq:.1f} Hz")
    print(f"Amplitude: {vpp:.3f} Vpp")

    # Capture waveform
    waveform = scope.acquire_waveform(1)
    waveform.plot("1 kHz Sine Wave")
finally:
    awg.disable_all_channels()
    awg.disconnect()
    scope.disconnect()

docs.title: Auto-Discovery
docs.text:
Let the toolkit find and connect to all instruments automatically.

from lab_instruments import find_all

instruments = find_all()

for name, inst in instruments.items():
    print(f"{name}: {type(inst).__name__} at {inst.resource_name}")

# Access by name
if "psu1" in instruments:
    psu = instruments["psu1"]
    psu.enable_output(True)
    psu.set_output_channel("positive_6_volts_channel", 5.0)

docs.title: SMU: IV Curve
docs.text:
Sweep voltage and measure current on the NI PXIe-4139 SMU.

import csv
from lab_instruments import NI_PXIe_4139

smu = NI_PXIe_4139("PXI1Slot2")
smu.connect()

voltages = [v * 0.1 for v in range(0, 34)]  # 0 to 3.3V in 100mV steps
results = []

try:
    smu.set_current_limit(0.1)
    smu.enable_output(True)

    for v in voltages:
        smu.set_voltage(v)
        result = smu.measure_vi()
        results.append({
            "voltage_set": v,
            "voltage_meas": result["voltage"],
            "current": result["current"],
            "compliance": result["in_compliance"],
        })
        print(f"{v:.1f} V -> {result['current']*1000:.3f} mA")

    # Export
    with open("iv_curve.csv", "w", newline="") as f:
        writer = csv.DictWriter(f, fieldnames=results[0].keys())
        writer.writeheader()
        writer.writerows(results)
finally:
    smu.enable_output(False)
    smu.disconnect()

docs.title: Multi-Instrument with Context Managers
docs.text:
from lab_instruments import HP_E3631A, HP_34401A, Rigol_DHO804

psu = HP_E3631A("GPIB0::5::INSTR")
dmm = HP_34401A("GPIB0::22::INSTR")
scope = Rigol_DHO804("USB0::0x1AB1::0x044C::DHO8A000000::INSTR")

psu.connect()
dmm.connect()
scope.connect()

try:
    with psu:
        psu.set_output_channel("positive_6_volts_channel", 5.0, current_limit=1.0)
        psu.enable_output(True)

        with dmm:
            dmm.configure_dc_voltage()
            voltage = dmm.read()
            print(f"DMM reads: {voltage:.6f} V")

        scope.enable_channel(1)
        scope.autoset()
        freq = scope.measure_frequency(1)
        vpp = scope.measure_vpp(1)
        print(f"Scope: {freq:.1f} Hz, {vpp:.3f} Vpp")
finally:
    psu.disconnect()
    dmm.disconnect()
    scope.disconnect()

docs.title: Data Collection with pandas
docs.text:
import time
import pandas as pd
from lab_instruments import HP_E3631A, HP_34401A

psu = HP_E3631A("GPIB0::5::INSTR")
dmm = HP_34401A("GPIB0::22::INSTR")
psu.connect()
dmm.connect()

data = []

try:
    psu.enable_output(True)
    dmm.configure_dc_voltage()

    for target in [1.0, 2.0, 3.3, 5.0]:
        psu.set_output_channel("positive_6_volts_channel", target, current_limit=0.5)
        time.sleep(0.5)

        # Take 5 readings at each voltage
        readings = [dmm.read() for _ in range(5)]
        for r in readings:
            data.append({"target_v": target, "measured_v": r})

    df = pd.DataFrame(data)
    summary = df.groupby("target_v")["measured_v"].agg(["mean", "std", "count"])
    print(summary)
    df.to_csv("measurements.csv", index=False)
finally:
    psu.enable_output(False)
    psu.disconnect()
    dmm.disconnect()

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: This guide covers how to use the lab_instruments Python package to control lab instruments programmatically.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
1. Python 3.10+
  2. NI-VISA — Download from ni.com. Required for USB and GPIB communication.
  3. PyVISA — Installed automatically with the package.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
pip install git+https://github.com/T-O-M-Tool-Oauto-Mationator/scpi-instrument-toolkit.git

For optional dependencies:

# NI PXIe-4139 SMU support
pip install nidcpower

# TI EV2300 USB-to-I2C support (Windows: automatic, Linux/macOS: pip install hidapi)

docs.title: Finding Your VISA Address
docs.text:
Every instrument needs a VISA resource string to connect. Common formats:

Interface Format Example USB USB0::VENDOR::PRODUCT::SERIAL::INSTR USB0::0x2A8D::0x1301::MY12345678::INSTR GPIB GPIB0::ADDRESS::INSTR GPIB0::22::INSTR Serial ASRL{N}::INSTR ASRL3::INSTR LAN TCPIP0::IP::INSTR TCPIP0::192.168.1.100::INSTR

To list connected instruments:

import pyvisa
rm = pyvisa.ResourceManager()
print(rm.list_resources())
# ('USB0::0x2A8D::0x1301::MY12345678::INSTR', 'GPIB0::22::INSTR', ...)

Or use the toolkit's auto-discovery:

from lab_instruments import find_all

instruments = find_all()
for name, inst in instruments.items():
    print(f"{name}: {type(inst).__name__} at {inst.resource_name}")

docs.title: Basic Pattern
docs.text:
All drivers follow the same lifecycle: instantiate, connect, use, disconnect.

from lab_instruments import HP_34401A

dmm = HP_34401A("GPIB0::22::INSTR")
dmm.connect()

try:
    dmm.configure_dc_voltage()
    voltage = dmm.read()
    print(f"Measured: {voltage} V")
finally:
    dmm.disconnect()

docs.title: Context Managers
docs.text:
All drivers support with statements for automatic cleanup:

from lab_instruments import HP_E3631A

psu = HP_E3631A("GPIB0::5::INSTR")
psu.connect()

with psu:
    psu.set_output_channel("positive_6_volts_channel", 3.3, current_limit=0.1)
    psu.enable_output(True)
    # ... do work ...
# Output is automatically disabled when exiting the `with` block

Note

Context managers handle safe shutdown (disabling outputs, zeroing setpoints). You still need to call connect() before entering the with block and disconnect() after.

docs.title: Imports
docs.text:
You can import directly from the top-level package:

from lab_instruments import HP_E3631A, HP_34401A, Rigol_DHO804

Or from the specific module:

from lab_instruments.src.hp_e3631a import HP_E3631A

Both forms are equivalent. The top-level import is recommended.

docs.title: Auto-Discovery
docs.text:
The InstrumentDiscovery class scans all connected VISA resources and returns typed driver instances:

from lab_instruments import InstrumentDiscovery

discovery = InstrumentDiscovery()
instruments = discovery.scan()

for name, inst in instruments.items():
    print(f"{name}: {type(inst).__name__}")
# psu1: HP_E3631A
# dmm1: HP_34401A
# scope1: Rigol_DHO804

Or use the shorthand:

from lab_instruments import find_all

instruments = find_all()

See Discovery for details.

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text:
Drivers raise standard Python exceptions:

import pyvisa
from lab_instruments import HP_34401A

dmm = HP_34401A("GPIB0::22::INSTR")

try:
    dmm.connect()
except pyvisa.VisaIOError as e:
    print(f"Connection failed: {e}")

# After connecting, check the instrument error queue:
error = dmm.get_error()
print(error)  # '+0,"No error"'
Exception When pyvisa.VisaIOError Connection failure, timeout, communication error ConnectionError Method called before connect() ValueError Invalid parameter (wrong channel, out-of-range value)

docs.title: Base Class: DeviceManager
docs.text:
All VISA-based drivers inherit from DeviceManager, which provides raw SCPI access. If your instrument isn't supported, you can use DeviceManager directly:

from lab_instruments import DeviceManager

inst = DeviceManager("GPIB0::10::INSTR")
inst.connect()

# Send raw SCPI commands
inst.send_command("*RST")
response = inst.query("*IDN?")
print(response)

inst.disconnect()

See DeviceManager for the full reference.

docs.title: DeviceManager
docs.text:
Base class for all VISA-based instrument drivers. Wraps PyVISA to provide raw SCPI communication.

from lab_instruments import DeviceManager

docs.title: Constructor
docs.text:
DeviceManager(resource_name)
Parameter Type Description resource_name str VISA resource string (e.g., "GPIB0::22::INSTR")

docs.title: Methods
docs.text: Method Description Returns connect() Open the VISA connection. Sets timeout to 5000 ms. None disconnect() Close the VISA connection. None send_command(command) Send a SCPI command (no response). None query(command) Send a SCPI command and return the response. str clear_status() Send *CLS to clear the status byte. None reset() Send *RST and *CLS to reset the instrument. None

docs.title: Properties
docs.text: Property Type Description resource_name str The VISA address passed to the constructor instrument pyvisa.Resource The underlying PyVISA resource (or None if not connected) rm pyvisa.ResourceManager The PyVISA ResourceManager instance

docs.title: Example: Raw SCPI
docs.text:
Use DeviceManager directly when your instrument isn't supported by a specific driver:

from lab_instruments import DeviceManager

inst = DeviceManager("USB0::0x1AB1::0x0588::DS1ZA000000000::INSTR")
inst.connect()

# Identify
print(inst.query("*IDN?"))

# Reset
inst.reset()

# Send custom commands
inst.send_command(":CHANnel1:DISPlay ON")
inst.send_command(":TIMebase:SCALe 0.001")

# Query a value
freq = inst.query(":MEASure:FREQuency? CHANnel1")
print(f"Frequency: {freq}")

inst.disconnect()

docs.title: Subclassing
docs.text:
To create a driver for a new instrument, subclass DeviceManager:

from lab_instruments.src.device_manager import DeviceManager

class MyInstrument(DeviceManager):
    def __init__(self, resource_name):
        super().__init__(resource_name)

    def measure_voltage(self):
        response = self.query("MEASURE:VOLTAGE?")
        return float(response.strip())

All built-in drivers follow this pattern. See any driver source file for a full example.

docs.title: InstrumentDiscovery & find_all
docs.text:
Auto-detect connected instruments and return typed driver instances.

from lab_instruments import InstrumentDiscovery, find_all

docs.title: find_all()
docs.text:
Convenience function that scans all VISA resources and returns a dictionary of detected instruments.

instruments = find_all()

Returns: dict[str, DeviceManager] — Keys are auto-assigned names (psu1, dmm1, scope1, etc.), values are connected driver instances.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
from lab_instruments import find_all

instruments = find_all()

for name, inst in instruments.items():
    print(f"{name}: {type(inst).__name__} at {inst.resource_name}")
# psu1: HP_E3631A at GPIB0::5::INSTR
# dmm1: HP_34401A at GPIB0::22::INSTR
# scope1: Rigol_DHO804 at USB0::0x1AB1::0x044C::DHO8A000000::INSTR

docs.title: InstrumentDiscovery
docs.text: Class for more control over the discovery process.

docs.title: Constructor
docs.text: discovery = InstrumentDiscovery()

docs.title: Methods
docs.text: Method Description Returns scan() Scan all VISA resources, identify instruments, return connected drivers dict[str, DeviceManager]

docs.title: Class Attributes
docs.text: Attribute Description MODEL_MAP dict mapping model substrings to driver classes NAME_MAP dict mapping model substrings to friendly names (psu, dmm, scope, awg, smu)

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
from lab_instruments import InstrumentDiscovery

discovery = InstrumentDiscovery()
instruments = discovery.scan()

# Access by auto-assigned name
psu = instruments.get("psu1")
if psu:
    psu.set_output_channel("positive_6_volts_channel", 5.0)

docs.title: Supported Model Detection
docs.text:
The discovery system identifies instruments by matching substrings in the *IDN? response:

Substring Driver Name E3631A HP_E3631A psu EDU36311A Keysight_EDU36311A psu MPS-6010H-1C MATRIX_MPS6010H psu 34401A HP_34401A dmm EDU34450A Keysight_EDU34450A dmm XDM1041 Owon_XDM1041 dmm EDU33212A Keysight_EDU33212A awg 4063 BK_4063 awg JDS6600 JDS6600_Generator awg DHO804 Rigol_DHO804 scope MSO2024 Tektronix_MSO2024 scope DSOX1204G Keysight_DSOX1204G scope PXIe-4139 NI_PXIe_4139 smu EV2300 TI_EV2300 ev2300

docs.title: BK Precision 4063
docs.text:
Dual-channel arbitrary waveform generator. Interface: USB.

from lab_instruments import BK_4063

docs.title: Quick Example
docs.text:
awg = BK_4063("USB0::0xF4EC::0x1101::SPD12345678::INSTR")
awg.connect()

try:
    awg.set_waveform(1, "SINE", frequency=1000, amplitude=2.0, offset=0)
    awg.enable_output(1, True)
finally:
    awg.disable_all_channels()
    awg.disconnect()

docs.title: Context Manager
docs.text:
awg = BK_4063("USB0::0xF4EC::0x1101::SPD12345678::INSTR")
awg.connect()

with awg:
    awg.set_waveform(1, "SQUARE", frequency=5000, amplitude=3.3, duty=50)
    awg.enable_output(1, True)
# Outputs disabled on exit

docs.title: Valid Waveforms
docs.text:
SINE, SQUARE, RAMP, PULSE, NOISE, DC, ARB

Aliases accepted: SIN, SQU, PULS, NOIS, TRI/TRIANGLE (mapped to RAMP).

docs.title: Output Control
docs.text: Method Description enable_output(channel, enabled=True) Enable/disable output for channel 1 or 2 disable_all_channels() Zero and disable both outputs set_output_impedance(channel, load) Set load impedance (ohms or "HZ" for high-Z) set_sync_output(channel, enabled) Enable/disable sync output

docs.title: Waveform Configuration
docs.text: Method Description set_waveform(channel, wave_type, frequency=None, amplitude=None, offset=None, phase=None, duty=None, symmetry=None) Set waveform type and all parameters set_dc_output(channel, voltage) Set DC output

docs.title: Modulation, Sweep & Burst
docs.text: Method Description set_modulation(channel, state, mod_type="AM", source="INT", **kwargs) Configure modulation (AM, FM, PM, FSK, ASK, DSBAM, PWM) set_sweep(channel, state, **kwargs) Frequency sweep (TIME, START, STOP, etc.) set_burst(channel, state, **kwargs) Burst mode (MODE, PRD, STPS, etc.)

docs.title: Query
docs.text: Method Returns Description get_amplitude(channel) float Current amplitude (Vpp) get_offset(channel) float Current DC offset get_frequency(channel) float Current frequency (Hz) get_output_state(channel) bool Whether output is enabled get_error() str Error queue

docs.title: Utility
docs.text: Method Description copy_channel(dest_channel, src_channel) Copy parameters between channels

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Uses BK Precision 4060 series command syntax (C1:BSWV WVTP,SINE,FRQ,1000,...).
  • The set_modulation, set_sweep, and set_burst methods accept keyword arguments that map directly to the instrument's parameter names.

docs.title: JDS6600
docs.text:
Dual-channel DDS function generator (up to 60 MHz). Interface: Serial (115200 baud).

from lab_instruments import JDS6600_Generator

Non-SCPI Protocol

The JDS6600 uses a proprietary ASCII protocol over USB serial, not standard SCPI. The driver handles all protocol details.

docs.title: Quick Example
docs.text:
awg = JDS6600_Generator("ASRL4::INSTR")
awg.connect()

try:
    awg.set_waveform(1, "sine")
    awg.set_frequency(1, 1000)      # 1 kHz
    awg.set_amplitude(1, 2.0)       # 2 Vpp
    awg.enable_output(ch1=True)
finally:
    awg.disable_output()
    awg.disconnect()

docs.title: Waveforms
docs.text:
17 built-in waveforms:

sine, square, pulse, triangle, partial_sine, cmos, dc, half_wave, full_wave, pos_ladder, neg_ladder, noise, exp_rise, exp_decay, multitone, sinc, lorenz

docs.title: Output Control
docs.text: Method Description enable_output(ch1=None, ch2=None) Enable/disable channels. None keeps current state. disable_output() Zero all parameters and disable both outputs

docs.title: Waveform Configuration
docs.text: Method Description set_waveform(channel, waveform) Set waveform type (see list above) set_frequency(channel, freq_hz) Set frequency in Hz (0.01 Hz to 60 MHz) set_amplitude(channel, amplitude_v) Set amplitude in Vpp (0.001 to ~20V) set_offset(channel, offset_v) Set DC offset (-10V to +10V) set_duty_cycle(channel, duty_percent) Duty cycle for pulse/square (0.1-99.9%) set_phase(channel, phase_deg) Set phase (0-359.9 degrees)

docs.title: Channel Synchronization
docs.text: Method Description set_sync(freq=False, waveform=False, amplitude=False, offset=False, duty=False) Sync CH2 to follow CH1 for selected parameters

docs.title: Query (Cached Values)
docs.text: Method Returns Description get_amplitude(channel) float or None Cached amplitude get_offset(channel) float or None Cached offset get_frequency(channel) float or None Cached frequency get_output_state(channel) bool Cached output state

docs.title: Dual-Channel Example
docs.text:
awg = JDS6600_Generator("ASRL4::INSTR")
awg.connect()

try:
    # CH1: 1 kHz sine, 2 Vpp
    awg.set_waveform(1, "sine")
    awg.set_frequency(1, 1000)
    awg.set_amplitude(1, 2.0)

    # CH2: 1 kHz square, 3.3 Vpp
    awg.set_waveform(2, "square")
    awg.set_frequency(2, 1000)
    awg.set_amplitude(2, 3.3)

    # Enable both
    awg.enable_output(ch1=True, ch2=True)
finally:
    awg.disable_output()
    awg.disconnect()

docs.title: Channel Sync Example
docs.text:
# Make CH2 mirror CH1's frequency and waveform
awg.set_sync(freq=True, waveform=True)

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Serial connection at 115200 baud, 8N1.
  • Query methods return cached values from the last set_* call, not live queries from the device.
  • Frequency resolution is 0.01 Hz for frequencies below 20 MHz.
  • The enable_output() method controls both channels simultaneously.

docs.title: Keysight EDU33212A
docs.text:
Dual-channel Trueform arbitrary waveform generator. Interface: USB, LAN.

from lab_instruments import Keysight_EDU33212A

docs.title: Quick Example
docs.text:
awg = Keysight_EDU33212A("USB0::0x2A8D::0x1602::MY12345678::INSTR")
awg.connect()

try:
    awg.set_waveform(1, "SIN", frequency=1000, amplitude=2.0, offset=0)
    awg.enable_output(1, True)
    # Signal is now outputting on CH1
finally:
    awg.disable_all_channels()
    awg.disconnect()

docs.title: Context Manager
docs.text:
awg = Keysight_EDU33212A("USB0::0x2A8D::0x1602::MY12345678::INSTR")
awg.connect()

with awg:
    awg.set_waveform(1, "SQU", frequency=5000, amplitude=3.3)
    awg.enable_output(1, True)
    # ...

docs.title: Channels
docs.text: Dual-channel: channel 1 and channel 2.

docs.title: Output Control
docs.text: Method Description enable_output(channel, enabled=True) Enable/disable output for channel 1 or 2 disable_all_channels() Zero all parameters and disable both outputs set_output_load(channel, load) Set output impedance: ohms (1-10000) or "INF" for high-Z set_output_polarity(channel, normal=True) Set normal or inverted polarity set_sync_output(enabled=True) Enable/disable Sync output

docs.title: Waveform Configuration

docs.title: HP 34401A
docs.text:
6.5-digit digital multimeter. Interface: GPIB.

from lab_instruments import HP_34401A

docs.title: Quick Example
docs.text:
dmm = HP_34401A("GPIB0::22::INSTR")
dmm.connect()

try:
    dmm.configure_dc_voltage()
    voltage = dmm.read()
    print(f"DC Voltage: {voltage:.6f} V")
finally:
    dmm.disconnect()

docs.title: Context Manager
docs.text:
dmm = HP_34401A("GPIB0::22::INSTR")
dmm.connect()

with dmm:
    dmm.configure_dc_voltage(range_val=10, nplc=10)
    reading = dmm.read()
    print(f"{reading:.6f} V")
# Instrument reset on exit

docs.title: Two Ways to Measure
docs.text:
Configure + Read (recommended for repeated measurements):

dmm.configure_dc_voltage()    # Configure once
reading1 = dmm.read()         # Trigger and read
reading2 = dmm.read()         # Read again (same config)

Immediate Measure (one-shot, reconfigures each time):

voltage = dmm.measure_dc_voltage()   # Configure + trigger + return

NPLC (Number of Power Line Cycles) controls integration time. Valid values: 0.02, 0.2, 1, 10, 100. Higher = less noise, slower.

docs.title: NPLC Example
docs.text:
dmm = HP_34401A("GPIB0::22::INSTR")
dmm.connect()

with dmm:
    # Fast measurement (20ms integration)
    dmm.configure_dc_voltage(nplc=0.02)
    fast = dmm.read()

    # High-accuracy measurement (2s integration at 50Hz mains)
    dmm.configure_dc_voltage(nplc=100)
    accurate = dmm.read()

    print(f"Fast: {fast:.6f} V")
    print(f"Accurate: {accurate:.6f} V")

dmm = HP_34401A("GPIB0::22::INSTR")

with dmm:

    dmm.set_sample_count(10)         # 10 samples per trigger
    reading = dmm.read()             # Returns average of 10 samples
    print(f"Average: {reading:.6f} V")

docs.title: OWON XDM1041
docs.text:
7.5-digit bench digital multimeter. Interface: USB (115200 baud serial).

from lab_instruments import Owon_XDM1041

Non-Standard SCPI

The XDM1041 uses a non-standard SCPI implementation:

  • Uses MEAS? instead of :READ?
  • Range is passed directly to CONFigure commands
  • SYSTem:ERRor? is not supported
  • Measurements require a 2-second settle delay (handled automatically)

docs.title: Quick Example
docs.text:
dmm = Owon_XDM1041("ASRL5::INSTR")
dmm.connect()

try:
    dmm.configure_dc_voltage()
    voltage = dmm.measure()
    print(f"DC Voltage: {voltage:.7f} V")
finally:
    dmm.disconnect()

docs.title: Context Manager
docs.text:
dmm = Owon_XDM1041("ASRL5::INSTR")
dmm.connect()

with dmm:
    voltage = dmm.measure_dc_voltage()
    print(f"{voltage:.7f} V")
# Instrument reset on exit

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: Method Description configure_dc_voltage(range_val=None) DC voltage. Range: 500e-3, 5, 50, 500, 1000 or None for auto configure_ac_voltage(range_val=None) AC voltage. Range: 500e-3, 5, 50, 500, 750 configure_dc_current(range_val=None) DC current. Range: 500e-6, 5e-3, 50e-3, 500e-3, 5, 10 configure_ac_current(range_val=None) AC current configure_resistance_2wire(range_val=None) 2-wire resistance. Range: 500, 5e3, 50e3, 500e3, 5e6, 50e6 configure_resistance_4wire(range_val=None) 4-wire resistance. Max 50kOhm configure_frequency() Frequency configure_period() Period configure_capacitance(range_val=None) Capacitance configure_temperature(rtd_type="KITS90") Temperature. RTD: "KITS90" (K-type) or "PT100" configure_diode() Diode test configure_continuity() Continuity test set_mode(mode) Set mode by name: vdc, vac, idc, iac, res, fres, freq, per, cap, temp, diod, cont

docs.title: Reading
docs.text: Method Returns Description measure() float Take a measurement using current config (2s settle) read() float Alias for measure() (compatibility)

docs.title: Immediate Measurement
docs.text:
Each method configures and measures in one call:

Method Returns measure_dc_voltage(range_val=None) float measure_ac_voltage(range_val=None) float measure_dc_current(range_val=None) float measure_ac_current(range_val=None) float measure_resistance_2wire(range_val=None) float measure_resistance_4wire(range_val=None) float measure_frequency() float measure_period() float measure_capacitance(range_val=None) float measure_temperature(rtd_type="KITS90") float measure_diode() float

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• The range_val=None parameter enables auto-range. Pass a specific value for fixed range.
  • All measurements have an inherent 2-second settle delay due to the device's processing time.
  • The serial connection uses 115200 baud, 8N1, with \r\n write termination.
  • 4-wire resistance is limited to a maximum range of 50 kOhm.

docs.title: HP E3631A
docs.text:
Triple-output DC power supply (+6V/5A, +25V/1A, -25V/1A). Interface: GPIB.

from lab_instruments import HP_E3631A

docs.title: Quick Example
docs.text:
psu = HP_E3631A("GPIB0::5::INSTR")
psu.connect()

try:
    psu.set_output_channel("positive_6_volts_channel", 3.3, current_limit=0.5)
    psu.enable_output(True)

    voltage = psu.measure_voltage("positive_6_volts_channel")
    current = psu.measure_current("positive_6_volts_channel")
    print(f"Output: {voltage:.3f} V, {current:.4f} A")
finally:
    psu.enable_output(False)
    psu.disconnect()

psu = HP_E3631A("GPIB0::5::INSTR")
psu.connect()

with psu:
    psu.set_output_channel("positive_25_volts_channel", 12.0, current_limit=0.2)
    psu.enable_output(True)
    # ...
# All channels zeroed and output disabled on exit

docs.title: Multi-Channel Example
docs.text:
psu = HP_E3631A("GPIB0::5::INSTR")
psu.connect()

with psu:
    # Set all three channels
    psu.set_output_channel("positive_6_volts_channel", 3.3, current_limit=1.0)
    psu.set_output_channel("positive_25_volts_channel", 12.0, current_limit=0.5)
    psu.set_output_channel("negative_25_volts_channel", -5.0, current_limit=0.2)
    psu.enable_output(True)

    # Measure each channel
    for ch in ["positive_6_volts_channel", "positive_25_volts_channel", "negative_25_volts_channel"]:
        v = psu.measure_voltage(ch)
        i = psu.measure_current(ch)
        print(f"{ch}: {v:.3f} V, {i:.4f} A")

docs.title: Matrix MPS-6010H
docs.text:
Single-channel DC power supply (60V/10A). Interface: Serial (9600 baud).

from lab_instruments import MATRIX_MPS6010H

No Readback

This device does not support query commands. All measure_* methods return cached setpoint values, not actual measured values. Use an external DMM for accurate measurements.

docs.title: Quick Example
docs.text:
psu = MATRIX_MPS6010H("ASRL3::INSTR")
psu.connect()

try:
    psu.set_voltage(12.0)
    psu.set_current_limit(1.0)
    psu.enable_output(True)

    # Returns cached setpoint, NOT measured value
    v = psu.measure_voltage()
    print(f"Setpoint: {v} V")
finally:
    psu.enable_output(False)
    psu.disconnect()

docs.title: Context Manager
docs.text:
psu = MATRIX_MPS6010H("ASRL3::INSTR")
psu.connect()

with psu:
    psu.set_output(24.0, 2.0)  # 24V, 2A limit
    psu.enable_output(True)
    # ...
# Output disabled and remote mode returned to local on exit

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Remote mode is automatically enabled on connect() and disabled on context manager exit.
  • The serial connection uses 9600 baud, 8 data bits, no parity, 1 stop bit.
  • Uses VOLT / CURR / OUTP command syntax (not standard SCPI VSET / ISET / OUTPUT).
  • To return to local (front panel) mode manually: psu.send_command("REM:OFF") or press Shift+7 on the instrument.

docs.title: NI PXIe-4139 (SMU)
docs.text:
Source Measure Unit: DC voltage/current source with measurement. Interface: PXI (via nidcpower).

from lab_instruments import NI_PXIe_4139

Optional Dependency

Requires the nidcpower package: pip install nidcpower. Also requires NI-DCPower driver software. If not installed, NI_PXIe_4139 will be None when imported.

Not SCPI-Based

This driver uses the NI-DCPower Python API, not PyVISA/SCPI. It does not inherit from DeviceManager.

docs.title: Quick Example
docs.text:
from lab_instruments import NI_PXIe_4139

smu = NI_PXIe_4139("PXI1Slot2")
smu.connect()

try:
    smu.set_voltage(3.3)
    smu.set_current_limit(0.1)
    smu.enable_output(True)

    result = smu.measure_vi()
    print(f"V: {result['voltage']:.4f} V")
    print(f"I: {result['current']:.6f} A")
    print(f"Compliance: {result['in_compliance']}")
finally:
    smu.enable_output(False)
    smu.disconnect()

docs.title: Context Manager
docs.text:
smu = NI_PXIe_4139("PXI1Slot2")
smu.connect()

with smu:
    smu.set_voltage(5.0)
    smu.set_current_limit(0.5)
    smu.enable_output(True)
    # ...
# Output disabled and zeroed on exit

docs.text: Parameter Value Voltage Range -60 V to +60 V Current Range -3 A to +3 A (DC) Max Source Power 20 W Max Sink Power 12 W Default Current Limit 10 mA

docs.text: Method Description set_source_delay(seconds) Set settle delay before measurement (0-167 s) get_source_delay() Get current source delay set_samples_to_average(n) Set averaging count for noise reduction get_samples_to_average() Get current averaging count

docs.title: Current Source Example
docs.text:
smu = NI_PXIe_4139("PXI1Slot2")
smu.connect()

with smu:
    # Source 100 mA, limit voltage to 5V
    smu.set_current_mode(0.1, voltage_limit=5.0)
    smu.enable_output(True)

    result = smu.measure_vi()
    print(f"V: {result['voltage']:.3f} V at 100 mA")

docs.title: Averaging Example
docs.text:
smu = NI_PXIe_4139("PXI1Slot2")
smu.connect()

with smu:
    smu.set_voltage(3.3)
    smu.set_current_limit(0.1)
    smu.set_samples_to_average(100)   # Average 100 samples
    smu.set_source_delay(0.01)        # 10 ms settle time
    smu.enable_output(True)

    v = smu.measure_voltage()
    i = smu.measure_current()
    print(f"{v:.6f} V, {i:.9f} A")

docs.title: TI EV2300 (USB-to-I2C)
docs.text:
USB-to-I2C/SMBus adapter for battery fuel gauge communication. Interface: USB HID.

from lab_instruments import TI_EV2300

Optional Dependency

Requires hidapi on Linux/macOS: pip install hidapi. On Windows, uses native HID APIs via ctypes. If unavailable, TI_EV2300 will be None when imported.

Not SCPI-Based

This driver communicates via USB HID protocol, not PyVISA/SCPI. It does not inherit from DeviceManager.

docs.title: Quick Example
docs.text:
from lab_instruments import TI_EV2300

ev = TI_EV2300()
ev.connect()

try:
    # Read a 16-bit word from I2C address 0x16, register 0x09
    result = ev.read_word(0x16, 0x09)
    if result["ok"]:
        print(f"Voltage: {result['value']} mV")
    else:
        print(f"Error: {result['error']}")
finally:
    ev.disconnect()

docs.title: Connection
docs.text: Method Description connect() Open the HID device disconnect() Close the HID device is_open() Check if device is connected

docs.title: SMBus Read Operations
docs.text: Method Returns Description read_word(i2c_addr, register) dict Read 16-bit word. Returns {"ok": bool, "value": int, "error": str} read_byte(i2c_addr, register) dict Read 8-bit byte read_block(i2c_addr, register) dict Read block of bytes. Returns {"ok": bool, "data": bytes, "length": int}

docs.title: SMBus Write Operations
docs.text: Method Returns Description write_word(i2c_addr, register, value) dict Write 16-bit word write_byte(i2c_addr, register, value) dict Write 8-bit byte write_block(i2c_addr, register, data) dict Write block of bytes send_byte(i2c_addr, command) dict Send single byte command

docs.title: Diagnostics
docs.text: Method Returns Description get_device_info() dict Device information (VID, PID, serial, etc.) probe_command(i2c_addr, start_reg, end_reg, ...) list[dict] Scan register range

docs.title: Static Methods
docs.text: Method Returns Description TI_EV2300.enumerate_devices() list[dict] List all connected EV2300 devices TI_EV2300.is_available() bool Check if any EV2300 is connected

docs.title: Return Value Format
docs.text:
All read/write operations return a dict with:

{
    "ok": True,       # Whether the operation succeeded
    "value": 1234,    # For read_word/read_byte: the integer value
    "data": b"\x01",  # For read_block: the raw bytes
    "length": 2,      # For read_block: number of bytes
    "error": "",      # Error message if ok=False
}

docs.title: Register Scan Example
docs.text:
ev = TI_EV2300()
ev.connect()

try:
    # Scan registers 0x00-0x1F on address 0x16 (standard SBS fuel gauge)
    results = ev.probe_command(0x16, 0x00, 0x1F)
    for r in results:
        if r["ok"]:
            print(f"Reg 0x{r['register']:02X}: {r['value']}")
finally:
    ev.disconnect()

docs.title: Battery Fuel Gauge Example
docs.text:
ev = TI_EV2300()
ev.connect()

try:
    GAUGE_ADDR = 0x16  # Standard SBS address

    # Read standard SBS registers
    voltage = ev.read_word(GAUGE_ADDR, 0x09)    # Voltage (mV)
    current = ev.read_word(GAUGE_ADDR, 0x0A)    # Current (mA, signed)
    soc = ev.read_word(GAUGE_ADDR, 0x0D)        # State of Charge (%)
    temp = ev.read_word(GAUGE_ADDR, 0x08)        # Temperature (0.1K)

    if voltage["ok"]:
        print(f"Voltage: {voltage['value']} mV")
    if current["ok"]:
        # Current is signed 16-bit
        raw = current["value"]
        signed = raw - 65536 if raw > 32767 else raw
        print(f"Current: {signed} mA")
    if soc["ok"]:
        print(f"SOC: {soc['value']}%")
    if temp["ok"]:
        temp_c = temp["value"] / 10.0 - 273.15
        print(f"Temperature: {temp_c:.1f} C")
finally:
    ev.disconnect()

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Cross-platform: Windows (native ctypes), Linux (/dev/hidrawN), macOS (hidapi or IOKit).
  • USB VID: 0x0451 (Texas Instruments), PID: 0x0036 (operational mode).
  • The EV2300 does not require any special drivers on Windows — it uses the built-in HID driver.
  • PEC (Packet Error Checking) is supported on some operations via the pec parameter.
